Kaz Kylheku is a software engineer and a notable member of the Esperanto language community. He is known for his contributions to the development of the Esperanto language technology, particularly in the field of natural language processing (NLP) and machine translation. Kylheku is also the developer of the Lernu Plu platform, a popular online tool for learning Esperanto.

Hans Hüttel is a Danish computer scientist and a professor at Aarhus University, Denmark. His research interests include formal methods, programming languages, and software engineering. Hüttel has made significant contributions to the development of process algebra, a formal method for modeling and analyzing concurrent and distributed systems. He has also worked on the design and analysis of programming languages, software verification, and program semantics. Hüttel has co-authored several books and research papers and is well-respected in the field of computer science.

Marta Luczak is a Polish woman who was sentenced to 30 years in prison for the murder of her 2-year-old son, Daniel Pelka, in the United Kingdom. Along with her partner, Mariusz Krezolek, they subjected the child to horrendous abuse and neglect over six months, culminating in his death from a head injury in March 2012.
